content: Pneumonia diagnosis requires: 1) Compatible clinical presentation (cough, fever, dyspnea, pleuritic chest pain); 2) Chest radiograph showing new infiltrate (required for definitive diagnosis); 3) Supporting labs (leukocytosis, elevated CRP/procalcitonin). CURB-65 assesses severity: Confusion, Urea >7 mmol/L, Respiratory rate ≥30/min, Blood pressure (systolic <90 or diastolic ≤60), age ≥65 (score 0-5; ≥2 suggests hospitalization). PSI/PORT score incorporates more variables for mortality prediction. Sputum Gram stain/culture indicated for severe CAP or HAP/VAP. Blood cultures recommended for severe CAP. Urinary antigens for Legionella/S. pneumoniae in select cases. CT chest if complications suspected or diagnosis uncertain.
